How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-10 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers where 2 or more coherent screeners converge within one trading day, with validated combos ranked first to filter single-signal noise.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CAT leads at score 190, hitting CLENOW_MOMENTUM, EUPHORIA, PULLBACK, and VARIANCE_PREMIUM across four screeners, making it the loudest raw signal in the batch. MS follows at 179 with the same momentum-pullback-premium cluster plus VOLUME_LEADER, which adds institutional-size confirmation that EUPHORIA alone does not provide. Score distribution is top-heavy: CAT and MS sit in the 179-190 band, BAND drops to 163 and PENG to 145, then seven names cluster tightly from 93 to 108, with 20 total overlaps across the full set. No ticker today matched a validated combo, so the entire batch carries multi-screener overlap conviction only, not the higher-confidence VALID-combo tier where the real backtest edges live. Macro is NEUTRAL at 49.25, offering no regime tailwind; first preference is MS at low conviction, where VOLUME_LEADER separates it from CAT and the EUPHORIA flag in CAT raises the risk of near-term exhaustion.

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: CAT (score 4, target no v2 estimate).

0 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Flags institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta, EMA8/21 trend alignment, and low-OI anomalies to identify high-conviction directional positioning.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1QQQ ↑50 Watch$716.40 -1.26%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape verdict is WATCH, not confirmed: 5 of 295 trades resolved with a 100% win rate and 200% avg return against SPY's 4.6%, but n=5 is too thin to validate or challenge the backtest's 68.1% win rate, and monthly cohort avg returns in the thousands of percent reflect options P&L rather than the backtest's 3.8% mean.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 295 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak. One name printed: QQQ at score 50, Watch tier, bullish sweep pattern, sitting below both the 65 and 80 conviction thresholds. The score distribution collapses to a single point, median equals max at 50, zero signals at 65-plus and zero at 80-plus. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 adds no directional support. If forced to act, QQQ long is the only name on the sheet, but at sub-65 score with a flat macro backdrop, sizing should be minimal.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the edge rather than contradicts it: 75% win rate and +3.8% average vs SPY at -0.8% over the same window, and the June cohort at 86% win rate and +4.7% average is the strongest month in the three-month lookback, consistent with the backtest Sharpe of 2.34.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PCG at 79 and EXC at 78 lead the batch, both utilities, which fits Sinclair's documented sector bias where utilities carry the highest structural variance premium (+31%). CL at 77 adds a consumer-staples name and is the only non-utility in the top three. The distribution is compressed: 37 of 41 signals sit between 65 and 79, median 70, and no name clears 80, so there is no high-conviction outlier today. NEUTRAL macro at 49.25 neither accelerates nor undermines variance premium selling. PCG first given top score, at measured conviction only, because the ceiling of 79 and zero names above 80 make this a thin batch rather than a strong one.

🧠 Identifies tickers where IV matches realized vol coherently, avoiding both catalyst-rich overpricing and market disbelief, surfacing cleaner directional setups.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

ULTA at 100 and PGR at 99 are the cleanest names in today's batch, both Very Strong tier and sitting at the screener ceiling with no pattern overlay to interpret. XLF at 97 adds a sector-level long that broadens the read beyond single-stock idiosyncrasy. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals score at or above 80, median sits at 75, and the upper tail is dense, not diffuse, which is a good sign for signal concentration. Macro is NEUTRAL at 49.25, offering neither a regime tailwind nor a reason to fade the batch, just a flat backdrop. Preference is ULTA first at high conviction, then PGR as the cleaner single-stock hedge to ULTA's consumer discretionary exposure.

🧠 Detects put-volume spikes above 3x 30-day avg in stable or rising stocks, flagging institutional hedging as a contrarian upside signal via IV crush.
Construction
Put volume > 3× avg(30d) + price stable or up. Kerberos thesis: contrarian - institutional hedging activity in calm tape often precedes upside continuation. Score weighs put vol ratio, price stability, IV crush.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is fully saturated: all 50 signals score exactly 100, making differentiation impossible within today's batch. AER, ALHC, and ALNY lead the listed picks but are identical in score and tier, and no pattern data is populated for any name. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 49.25) does not actively suppress this thesis, but it provides no tailwind either. Scoring saturation at this scale - every signal maxing out - suggests either a screener calibration issue or a day when the put-vol trigger fired indiscriminately across the tape, and this batch should be treated as noisy. If forced to pick one name first, AER in capital-intensive aviation leasing is a reasonable starting point for manual review, but conviction is low given zero score differentiation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record at 65.3% win rate and +4.5% average against SPY's +3.7% confirms the backtest verdict, though February's cohort at 24.6% win rate and -3.3% average shows the strategy breaks sharply when the broader tape deteriorates, making the current NEUTRAL regime the key risk to watch.

FULL SIZE OKWR 64% · avg +4.3% · beats SPY +4.1% by +0.2 pts

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 497 closed · WR TB 49% · 20% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ZGN leads at score 90 (Very strong, LAUNCH), the only name at the ceiling and the clearest expression of the setup today. CDNS and CSCO both score 85 (Strong, TRIGGER) - CDNS carries higher-quality trend credentials as a large-cap EDA franchise, CSCO offers lower-beta exposure if the NEUTRAL macro keeps risk appetite contained. The score distribution is compressed to the point of near-uselessness for differentiation: 48 of 50 signals land at or above 80, median sitting at 80, which means the screener is flagging a crowded setup day rather than isolating true standouts. A NEUTRAL macro regime (49.25) provides no tailwind but does not break pullback reversals, which have absorbed mixed tapes reasonably in the 90-day live track. Start with ZGN at highest conviction given the LAUNCH pattern, add CDNS as the secondary at half size.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LAC, PPTA, and SMR all score 94 Very strong, making them the strongest entries today, though pattern data is null across every name in the batch, so conviction rests purely on score and tier. IDCC and TMC follow at 89 Strong and are the next tier down worth tracking. The distribution is tight and top-heavy: median 83, 15 of 27 names at 80 or above, all 27 clearing 65, which reads as a clean batch rather than a thin or scattered one. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 49.25, which neither amplifies nor kills a mean-reversion setup, but it removes any macro tailwind from the trade thesis. Take LAC first among the three-way tie at 94, sizing it as a basket leg at moderate conviction consistent with how this edge is built, not as a single-name directional bet.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile plus VIX term structure and FOMC proximity, targeting contrarian sell-vol entries when fear peaks.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑63 Watch$753.58 -0.18%SELL VOL1.53
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener lives or dies by tier - Signal prints (n=7) run at 85.7% WR and +2.73% avg while Watch prints (n=20) limp in at 60% WR and +1.03% avg, and the June cohort's +0.18% avg reveals the edge softening materially from April's exceptional +5.43%, so today's Watch-only batch arriving into a weakening monthly trend demands low size.

Today delivers one name: SPY at score 61, Watch tier, LONG, with no pattern attached. The score distribution is as thin as it gets - one signal, nothing clearing 65 or 80. 🧠 Detects stocks with options volume above 5x 20-day mean, calm price action, and premium over $50K, targeting informed positioning before moves.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day resolved sample (18 of 620 signals, 100% win rate) and four months of cohort data (all at 100% win rate) appear to confirm the backtest, but the key number is 18 - too few resolved trades to clear the WATCH verdict's own 20-trade minimum, so the backtest's 61% win rate and 0.097 Bonferroni p-value remain the only load-bearing statistics.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HIMS and HUT both score 79 and sit at the top of a 20-signal batch, but they carry conflicting pattern signals - HUT's BULLISH SWEEP aligns with its LONG direction making it the cleaner read, while HIMS flags a BEARISH SWEEP against a LONG bias which cuts the conviction. GIS at 77 also runs a BULLISH SWEEP/LONG pairing and qualifies as a secondary candidate. The score distribution is soft: median at 59, nothing above 80, only 7 of 20 signals clear 65, which is a thin ceiling for this screener on this batch. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (49.25), which does not kill flow trades but removes any directional tailwind. HUT is the first name at moderate conviction only, GIS second; pass on HIMS until the pattern and direction agree.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLC is the one name worth attention, scoring 95 and the only reading above 80 across the full 11-ETF universe. XLV and XLF both reach 65 and cross into Signal tier, giving them secondary interest, though neither carries a defined pattern to anchor the trade. The score distribution is compressed and top-heavy, with four names pinned at 55 and a median of 60, which reads as cluster noise rather than broad opportunity. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 provides no sector rotation tailwind and argues for restraint. XLC is the highest-conviction name in today's batch, but the absence of any pattern flag means entering at partial size is the right posture rather than full commitment.

🧠 Flags stocks off 40 percent or more from 52-week highs with capitulation volume and a reversal candle, targeting mean reversion as sellers exhaust.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (62% WR, avg +8.1% versus SPY +3.3%, verdict KEEP) confirms the backtest's 63% win rate, but the monthly cohorts flag the key risk - January 2026 averaged -12.9% and February -5.8%, showing this screener bleeds when macro turns hostile, which the current NEUTRAL regime at 49.25 does not rule out.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RGC (score 100, Very strong) and HTZ (92, Very strong) lead today's 50-name batch, both flagging breakout patterns after deep drawdowns. ELTX at 88 rounds out the top tier with the same structure. The distribution sits on a high floor - all 50 signals clear 65 and the median is 73, but only six top 80, leaving the Very strong tier thin. Macro is NEUTRAL at 49.25, which neither amplifies nor discourages capitulation setups and makes this stock-specific rather than regime-driven. Take RGC first at high conviction given the perfect score, with HTZ as the follow-on name.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by 90-day regression slope times R-squared, filtered on price above MA100 and a SPY/MA200 regime gate to control trend quality.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HUM is the only name worth taking seriously today, the sole Very strong tier entry at a score of 100 out of 17 signals. PANW at 57 is next but lands in Watch, a tier where the 90-day data shows a 5.3% win rate and an average return of -5.7%. The score distribution is extreme, with a median of 10 and only one name clearing 65, so nearly the whole batch sits below any actionable threshold. Macro is NEUTRAL at 49.25, which keeps the SPY/MA200 gate open but does not rescue a thin tape. Take HUM first and only, at high conviction relative to everything else in this batch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is two names: NKE at a perfect score of 100 in the Very strong tier, and GIS at 77 in the Signal tier. With only 2 signals and a median of 77, the distribution is barely a batch - thin does not begin to cover it. NEUTRAL macro (score 49.25) offers no directional push either way. The uncomfortable fact is that 90-day live tracking shows the Very strong tier posting a 30% win rate and -11.87% average return, the worst of any tier, which makes NKE's top score a flashing yellow, not green. GIS in the Signal tier looks comparatively better given the live tier hierarchy. Prefer GIS first at very low conviction, and hold NKE until the tier inversion in live data is resolved or sample size grows.

🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more major intermarket pairs show |z|>=2 seven-day correlation breaks, betting on macro mean reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

🧠 Captures stocks drifting higher after a positive EPS surprise plus gap-up, exploiting slow analyst revision propagation and delayed institutional repositioning.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$10.61 -3.33%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 57%, avg +3.9%, beats SPY by +3.7 pts) supports KEEP, but the June 2026 cohort (n=89, WR 37%, avg -0.13%) is a sharp deterioration that contradicts the trailing read and is the number that matters right now.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BB is the only name in today's batch, scoring 95 (Very strong, PRIMED) - one signal at the ceiling is not a distribution, it is a data point. The surprise magnitude, gap hold, and revision activity that drive that score are individually compelling, but a single-name batch gives you no peer confirmation. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 49.25) does not actively fight PEAD continuation, but it does not amplify it either. Take BB first at moderate conviction and size conservatively given the batch is about as thin as it gets.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live 90-day tracking at 76% WR and a +5.6% avg that beats SPY by 2.9 points confirms the KEEP verdict, but the monthly cohort trend shows fragility: May 2026 collapsed to 49% WR and -1.1% avg before June recovered to 69% WR and +3.9%, meaning the edge is regime-dependent rather than consistent, and the key number is that -1.1% May avg.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score 100 today, with a median of 100 and every name sitting above 80, so the score distribution is entirely ceiling-compressed and offers no ranking signal. Patterns are null across every pick, which strips the usual differentiation layer and forces sector logic: APLD stands out as an AI-infrastructure name with an active macro narrative, and AGCO offers agricultural-equipment exposure at a time when commodity-adjacent equities have been quietly catching bids. AMZU adds a high-beta leveraged-Amazon structure for traders who want duration on that cross. The macro regime reads NEUTRAL at 49.25, neither supporting nor opposing a momentum batch. With scores and patterns both flat, preference goes to APLD first, at moderate conviction only.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms the screener at 61% WR and +5.4% avg vs SPY +1.3% (KEEP verdict), and the monthly cohort trend supports it with avg returns improving from -0.73% in April to +4.9% in June, but both sources contradict a naive reading of the scores - the Very strong tier has run at only 33% WR and -2.52% avg over the same 90 days, the worst performing tier in the dataset, so today's top-scored names carry the most caution.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACHC, ANAB, BEAM, and BV are tied at the top with scores of 95 in the Very strong tier, all flagged PRIMED, alongside CCCC, CRSP, and DFH at 90, also Very strong. ABEO at 85 is the lone Strong-tier name in today's batch. All 50 signals clear the 65 threshold, 18 of 50 hit 80 or above, and the median sits at 75, making this a broad and well-populated batch with real density at the top. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 provides no tailwind for squeeze ignition, which depends on risk appetite and active participation. Given 90d tier data showing the Very strong tier posts the worst outcomes of any tier at 33% WR and -2.52% avg while the Strong tier runs at 66% WR and +6.4% avg, ABEO at 85 is the first preference today, at moderate conviction.

🧠 Ranks Russell 1000 stocks by relative volume across three timeframes multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum, flagging names where volume surge confirms trend.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DAL, META, and MRNA share the max score of 100, but no pattern tags exist for any of the 50 names, removing the one qualitative filter that might differentiate them. The score distribution is entirely ceiling-compressed: median 96, all 50 names above 80, zero internal dispersion - the screener cannot rank within today's batch. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 provides no directional lift for a pure long-momentum approach. With live tracking at KILL and no pattern signal to lean on, batch conviction is low; META at 100 is the cleanest liquid name if a position is required, but size should reflect that this screener is flagged for retirement.

Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape directly contradicts the old Sharpe-3.4 narrative: win rate sits at 37% with an avg 20-day return of -7.7% versus SPY at -1.4%, a -6.3-point lag, and monthly cohorts confirm a clean deterioration from April avg +8.4% to May +2.6% to June -1.6%, making the KILL verdict the only honest read of this screener right now.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 adds no directional tailwind and is not a reason to lean hard into 1-10 DTE longs. CWAN first given the score gap, but conviction must be calibrated against the screener's live track record, not its old unrigorous backtest.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Rejection-tail bars (>=1.8x ATR) at Market Profile value-area extremes flag mean-reversion entries where price pushed outside the distribution and got rejected.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $TROO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1TROO ↑100●●● Very strong$2.19 +5.80%LOW2.2726.4%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one name, TROO, scoring a perfect 100 (Very strong, LONG), with pattern detail null so the specific tail configuration is unverifiable from the feed. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (49.25), which provides no tailwind for a mean-reversion long thesis and no reason to dismiss it either. 🧠 Catches post-earnings drift continuation entries using a weekly pullback-and-reclaim trigger within the first four weeks after a gap-up reaction.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW signals by excluding entries when KVO or Force Index show conflicting crosses within 3 days, using volume divergence as a negative gate to remove noise.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $XRT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1XRT ↑64 Watch$89.36 +1.45%BULLISH SWEEPCALL87.5$374KOPENING188167STRONG33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak: one name only, XRT at 64 (Watch, LONG, BULLISH SWEEP), failing to clear the 65 threshold and sitting in the lowest qualifying tier. A single-signal day where max equals median at 64 is not a distribution, it is a flat line with no dispersion to rank against. The NEUTRAL macro regime (49.25) gives no tailwind to a borderline retail-sector long, and zero signals in the top two tiers strips any case for pressing size. If forced to act, XRT first and only, at minimal conviction, with no add until the score breaks above 65 on a subsequent refresh.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record (39.8% win rate, avg -4.4% vs SPY -0.8%) and a June cohort that cratered to 30% win rate and -9.5% avg both confirm rather than contradict the backtest REJECT verdict - the key number is the -3.6-point SPY spread that earned the KILL call, with the monthly trend showing clear and accelerating deterioration since April.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AGL is the only name above 65, scoring a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier, but that tier's live 90-day record sits at 36.8% win rate and -4.3% average return, which is a poor host for today's top print. ABVX lands at 52 in Watch as the distant second, with the remaining six names bunched between 27 and 36. Score distribution is extreme and top-heavy: median of 21 across 35 signals, one name clearing 80, one clearing 65. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 49.25) removes the kind of SPY tailwind that made April cohorts work. AGL is the only name worth touching today, at low conviction, given its tier's live underperformance and null pattern data across all picks.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AMD, PANW, and DDOG score 100 and sit in the 'Very strong' tier, as do all 50 names in the batch, meaning the model has stacked every signal at the ceiling and offers no rank differentiation. Score distribution is fully compressed: median 99, all 50 above 80, which makes the scores decorative rather than informative. The batch is concentrated in semis and tech infrastructure, sector exposure that fits neither a NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 nor a screener carrying a KILL verdict. No name earns a first look at any conviction level.

🧠 Flags US equities where weekly RSI(14) drops below 30, then stacks daily RSI crossover, candlestick reversal, and zone scoring to find oversold bounce setups.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $DKL $BORR $FANG
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSI (D)RSI (W)CrossoverPattern
1DKL ↑66● Signal$54.70 +0.90%37.77—YESENGULFING
2BORR ↑63 Watch$4.42 +2.08%40.00—YESENGULFING
3FANG ↑61 Watch$188.36 +2.71%23.95—NOHAMMER
4CAPR ↑60 Watch$20.90 -2.84%15.2429.80NOHAMMER
5CRGY ↑59 Watch$9.82 +3.75%35.1828.45YESHAMMER
6XOM ↑58 Watch$142.58 +2.66%16.78—NOHAMMER
7AGI ↑57 Watch$28.84 -2.09%33.1428.71YESDRAGONFLY
8EGY ↑53 Watch$5.41 +3.03%19.60—NOHAMMER
9EOG ↑53 Watch$137.46 +2.51%18.42—NOENGULFING
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No valid backtest exists (doctrine-rejected at N=8 with null Sharpe), so the 90-day live tape is the only anchor: 54.7% win rate and +4.5% avg at 20 days beat SPY by 4.4 points (KEEP), but the monthly cohort win rate has fallen from 78.7% in March to 54.2% in June, with May printing a negative avg of -0.44%, signaling the edge is compressing in recent tape rather than confirming any prior stat.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and OI delta in crypto-linked ETFs, weighting flow intensity against spot crypto correlation to score directional conviction.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $MARA $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1MARA ↑50 Watch$12.57 -0.24%45.26-2.6%neutral
2MSTR ↑50 Watch$92.25 -2.53%40.63-17.9%neutral
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

NFRA and CLOU share the top score at 71, both Signal-tier longs, though neither carries a pattern tag so the precise setup driving the score is opaque. IDNA at 70 is the third-ranked name, and notably every name in today's batch is an ETF rather than a single stock, which dilutes the precision of any theme call. The score distribution is compressed and low-ceiling - median 53, max only 71, zero names clearing 80, and just 7 of 50 clearing 65 - this is a middling, uninspiring batch. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 offers no tailwind to push through weak scores, and the absence of any name in a high-conviction tier argues for standing aside. If forced to act, NFRA first as infrastructure theme positioning, at low conviction only.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names printed at score 100 today - BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, TNDM - all Very strong, all LONG, no pattern data on any of them, so the screener provides no internal ranking to work from. SRTY is a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF appearing as a LONG signal alongside direct equity longs, which complicates reading this batch as a clean momentum setup. The score distribution is mid-weight: 50 total signals, median 70, only 12 at 80 or above, and 82% clearing the 65 threshold, neither thin nor sharply top-heavy. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 adds no tailwind. With identical scores and no pattern data across all eight top names, there is no data-driven basis for preferring one; pass on forced ranking here and wait for a batch with score or pattern separation.

🧠 Scores stocks by hedge fund accumulation breadth across 20 top 13F filers, weighting new positions at +20 and increases at +10 against closes and trims.
Construction

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and COE both score a perfect 100 and lead the rank; JEF at 98 is the most liquid and institutionally recognizable name in the batch, making it the easiest to diligence quickly. Pattern data is null across the board, so the only differentiator is the composite score itself. The distribution is unusually compressed: all 46 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 94, meaning the screener is not sorting today so much as endorsing the entire pool at once, which dilutes any ranking signal. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 is not a tailwind here, since insider buy signals tend to pay out best when the broad tape carries a directional bid. Start with JEF at low conviction given liquidity and verifiable business context, and hold SVRE and COE as speculative watches pending diligence on transaction size and insider rank specifics.

🧠 Ranks the cheapest non-financial US equities by EV/EBIT ascending, scoring 100 the lowest-multiple name each day to capture mean-reversion of depressed operating earnings.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Zero of 69 signals have resolved in the 90-day window (need at least 20) and no backtest exists yet, so the 56.7% tiebreaker win rate on 30 names is the only number available and it is too thin to confirm or contradict any edge claim.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

BZ tops the batch at a perfect 100 and WRD sits right behind at 97, both flagged Very strong with no pattern qualifier, meaning the edge here is purely the multiple compression thesis rather than any momentum overlay. KROS at 93 is the third Very strong name and rounds out the highest-conviction tier. The score distribution is top-heavy: 7 of 16 signals print at 80 or above and 11 clear 65, with a median of 73, so this is not a day where the screener is scraping the barrel. Macro is NEUTRAL at 49.25, which is not the ideal hunting ground for deep value re-ratings but is not an active headwind either. First preference is BZ at moderate conviction, with WRD as the close second, both sized as starter positions until the screener builds a resolved-trade track record.

🧠 Flags stocks where 3 or more insiders buy within 30 days, scoring on cluster size, dollar value, and C-suite rank weight.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Finds stocks lagging within already-moving sectors, betting intra-sector rotation compresses the relative performance gap over the following weeks.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 SECTOR_PICKS scores top relative-strength tickers inside bullish sectors, combining 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster ranking to surface momentum leaders.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$25.27 +0.62%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$81.62 -0.04%Financials9.2%52.1%
3ABCL ↑74● Signal$6.52 -4.12%Healthcare7.8%47.3%
4A ↑65● Signal$133.47 -0.61%Healthcare15.9%17.1%
5ABCB ↑56 Watch$90.12 -0.32%Financials4.9%15.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks with retail call-volume spikes, Reddit mention surges, and parabolic price moves lacking an earnings catalyst as short candidates.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $AR $CC $CF
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1AR ↓100●●● Very strong$33.62 +1.17%FOMO1.28-1.2%
2CC ↓100●●● Very strong$18.45 +3.59%FOMO1.010.9%
3CF ↓100●●● Very strong$120.20 +2.81%FOMO1.360.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no backtest to compare against, the live tape stands alone - the 90-day WR of 56% at -0.25% average beats SPY by only 21 basis points, the trade-book WR collapses to 31% with 15% of options expiring worthless, and June's cohort WR falling to 47% at -2.76% average from May's 62.5% all confirm the KILL verdict rather than build a case for patience.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=2 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Experimental composite scoring volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and options flow, seeking edge where implied diverges from realized.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $SVXY $UVXY $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1SVXY ↓85●● Strong$58.32 -0.37%30%524%RULE_1
2UVXY ↓85●● Strong$23.46 +1.23%80%571%RULE_1
3VXX ↓85●● Strong$21.30 +0.80%46%349%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tape refutes the premise: 27 resolved trades produce a 26% win rate and avg return of -9.2% against SPY at -0.4%, a -8.8 point gap, with the May 2026 cohort (n=24, the only statistically meaningful sample) anchoring that underperformance, and April's single trade at -24.6% showing the tail risk is real.

Today's batch surfaces two names: SVXY and VXX, both scoring 85 (Strong tier, NEUTRAL direction, pattern data null on both). Flagging the long-vol proxy and its structural inverse at identical scores in the same session is incoherent - it signals the experimental scoring is misfiring, not that two real setups exist simultaneously. Score distribution is trivially thin - n=2, max equals median at 85, both above 80, so there is no spread to rank by. NEUTRAL macro (score 49.25) offers no directional cover for either side. With the live KILL verdict active, neither name earns a position today at any conviction.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects stocks where 25-delta put/call skew surges above the 90-day baseline, reading the hedging demand spike as contrarian fuel for a long reversal.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $SBUX $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1SBUX ↓65● Signal$107.11 +1.04%41%810.09SELL PUT (put skew rich)
2MSTR ↑61 Watch$92.25 -2.53%66%990.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no validated backtest on record (N=10, well short of the 30-trade minimum), the 90-day live tape is the only evidence base, and it reads KEEP at 55% WR and +7.5% avg beating SPY by 6.3 points, but the critical distortion is a tier inversion where Watch signals (67% WR, +13.9% avg) drastically outperform Very Strong signals (22% WR, -4.9%), and June's cohort has since dropped to -2.6% avg, both of which suggest the score ranking is not yet a reliable proxy for forward returns.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies failed breakouts above the value area high that close back inside, trapping late longs and flagging a short-side entry signal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AROC $AEE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AROC ↓60 Watch$38.91 +0.97%BULL_TRAP40.6936.74
2AEE ↓55 Watch$114.02 +0.95%BULL_TRAP113.80109.17
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no formal backtest to anchor against, the 90-day tape is the only scorecard, and it reads KILL - overall WR of 57.5% looks passable but the Watch tier averages -3.4% P&L, and monthly cohorts flip from 33% WR in May to 63% in June, confirming noise rather than a durable edge.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV dominates call IV above the 90th percentile, capturing unusual put demand that can signal imminent directional moves.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$390.19 -2.45%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Nine resolved trades return a 55.56% raw win rate but a -5.70% average 20-day gain against SPY's +0.77%, a gap dragged entirely by the Strong tier's two trades averaging -55.92%, and the most recent June 2026 cohort posted 0% wins on two names, so the live tape does not yet confirm the screener's edge.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is one name: AVGO at score 100, Very Strong tier, flagged LONG off extreme bearish skew. The LONG direction on a bearish-skew screen is a contrarian read, treating extreme put demand as overcrowding that favors a reversion trade. A single signal with both max and median at 100 is not a distribution, it is a lone data point, and the screener is running thin. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 provides no directional push for this kind of vol-skew play. Take AVGO first at moderate conviction, given the Very Strong tier has averaged +7.99% at 75% win rate over the 90-day window, the best subgroup result in this dataset.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices the realized move implied by Yang-Zhang HV20, targeting long vol into the event.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $NEE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1NEE ↑55 Watch$88.30 +0.38%PRE_IV_RAMP11—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name - HON at score 55, Watch tier, no pattern tag. Nothing clears 65; the entire distribution sits at one mid-tier reading, which is a thin, weak output. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 49.25 is indifferent to long vol plays, neither confirming nor blocking the setup. HON is the only name to watch but at Watch conviction, not Confirm, it is not actionable today - monitor for a score upgrade closer to the event.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags individual tickers where IV30 minus HV20 spikes above 1.5 sigma on a 90-day window, surfacing inflated fear premiums for short-vol trades.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1SPY ↓61 Watch$753.58 -0.18%23%15%1.54FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The monthly cohort trend contradicts the full-period headline: April ran 55.9% win rate and +10.8% avg, May slipped to 42% and -0.22%, June fell to 33.3% and -3.4%, a clear three-month deterioration that makes the aggregate 43% WR and +1.4% avg look stale and masks a worsening recent edge.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags closes that breach VAL or VAH then immediately reclaim, trapping directional traders and triggering mean-reversion entries.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AGCO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AGCO ↑60 Watch$114.89 +0.50%BEAR_TRAP115.01117.723.75
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape firmly confirms the structural edge: a 73% win rate and +5.85% avg return over 20 days versus SPY at -0.38% is a +6.2-point beat, and monthly cohorts held 77% in May and 71% in June, showing no decay in the signal.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screener construction is undocumented in the supplied data; the mechanism cannot be characterized from available fields alone.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

🧠 VIX Term-Structure Basis screens the spread between VIX3M and spot VIX to identify complacency via contango or fear via backwardation in equity vol markets.
Construction
VIX futures basis = VIX3M minus VIX. Contango = complacency, backwardation = fear. See Volatility Desk for the live read.

🧠 STFR is an EOD screener; construction logic is undocumented here, so the edge mechanism cannot be independently described.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA identifies institutional supply and demand imbalances by reading price spread, volume, and close position relative to the bar range.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.

Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
